jobs.purpose: Create the tenant's is_default=true Organization on tenancy::TenantProvisioned. Runs once per tenant. Idempotent: no-op when a default already exists.
steps: Guard: fail-fast if the tenant already has an is_default=true row (idempotent re-dispatch safety).
Read tenant.name + tenant.business_type.
Infer category via config('organization.provisioning.category_map').
INSERT organizations row: name=<tenant.name>, slug='default' (via reserved-slug bypass), is_default=true, parent_id=null, category=<inferred>, status=active, sort_order=0.
Consume 1 unit of organization_slot entitlement.
Fire OrganizationCreated (was_seeded=true) + TenantDefaultOrganizationSeeded.

jobs.purpose: After a parent_id change on an Organization, walk the moved subtree BFS + recompute tree_path + tree_depth for every descendant. Blocks the tree cache invalidation until completion.
steps: Load the moved Organization + its new tree_path (already updated in the reparent transaction by the observer).
Enumerate every descendant via `WHERE tree_path LIKE '.<old_prefix>.%'` (querying against the OLD tree_path snapshot captured in the event payload).
For each descendant: compute new tree_path = <moved_new_prefix>.<descendant_old_suffix>. Also update tree_depth = count(tree_path splits) - 2 (accounts for sentinel dots).
Batch-update in chunks of 100 rows within a single transaction per chunk (avoids holding row-locks on the whole subtree).
Invalidate the tag 'organization-tree' on the affected tenant.
Fire an internal `RebuildTreePathsJobCompleted` event so downstream caches (branch scope, teams scope) can refresh.
notes: Uniqueness prevents duplicate concurrent rebuilds for the same node — a rapid reparent-then-revert would otherwise fire two overlapping jobs.
Rebuild is idempotent: rerunning against the same node produces identical output.

jobs.purpose: Nightly sweep. Verifies EXACTLY ONE is_default=true row per tenant. Repairs drift when config('organization.reconciliation.repair_mode')='auto'; escalates otherwise.
steps: SELECT tenant_id, COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE is_default=true) FROM organizations GROUP BY tenant_id HAVING COUNT(*) != 1.

For each violating tenant, decide the resolution: zero defaults → promote the oldest active row (or dispatch SeedDefaultOrganizationForTenantJob when zero rows exist); multiple defaults → keep the oldest is_default=true row, demote the rest.
Fire OrganizationDefaultChanged(source='reconciler') for every repair.
Emit the `academorix.organization.default_invariant.violations` counter with the tenant_id label pattern for observability.

jobs.purpose: Daily. Hard-delete archived Organizations past the 730-day hold window IF no descendants + no branch references remain.
steps: SELECT id, tenant_id FROM organizations WHERE deleted_at < NOW() - INTERVAL '730 days'.

For each candidate: verify (a) no descendants (JOIN organizations children ON children.parent_id = id — zero rows required), (b) no branches referencing (JOIN branches ON branches.organization_id = id — zero rows required).
When both checks pass: hard-delete + fire OrganizationDeleted with the terminal-purge payload.
When either check fails: leave the row archived (retention held for another sweep) + emit `academorix.organization.purge_blocked` metric with reason label.
